entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The legume crop being bred for early maturity and high yield.

entities.name: EARLINESS
entities.description: The characteristic of early maturity in peanut cultivars.

entities.name: THERMAL TIME CONCEPT
entities.description: The theoretical approach of using heat accumulation to determine crop maturity.

entities.name: CUMULATIVE THERMAL TIME
entities.description: A procedure using predetermined degree-day accumulations to select for early-maturing cultivars.

entities.name: HIGH YIELD
entities.description: The production capacity of the peanut cultivars selected alongside earliness.

entities.name: MATURITY-RELATED TRAITS
entities.description: Traits associated with the physiological maturity of the crop.

entities.name: NO-STRESS ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: The environmental condition under which selections for yield and maturity were made.

entities.name: ICRISAT CENTER, PATANCHERU, INDIA
entities.description: The site where meteorological records were used to determine CTT values.

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The specific growth period from mid June to mid October used for the study.
